articles.summary: IRGC warns states hosting US forces of 'corresponding response' Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has warned countries hosting US military forces to prepare for a "corresponding response", Iranian media reported. In a statement carried by Tasnim News Agency, the IRGC urged those countries to "activate their civil defence units to protect their citizens and move them away from potential military targets". The Guard said the countries were being used as "launchpads for aggression against Iran". The IRGC also claimed it had used drones and missiles to target a US military logistics

articles.summary: Iran media: Blasts in Khorramabad, US assets in Bahrain struck Iranian media have reported that US missile strikes launched from Kuwait caused explosions in the city of Khorramabad in Lorestan province. Here are some other developments on Saturday: 'Bahrain targets hit': According to an Iranian military statement reported by the Tasnim news agency, Iranian missiles targeted US military assets and fuel tanks at the Sheikh Isa airbase and several communication bridges in Bahrain. The statement said the US had used the base to launch “operations against regional targets, especially our country”.

This brings the number of Lebanese schools destroyed in Israeli strikes to 20. Unesco said last month that 17 schools have been destroyed and more than 100 damaged in Lebanon since Israel launched its military campaign. articles.summary: Report: Nearly a quarter of Israelis considering leaving if Netanyahu is re-elected About 23 percent of Israelis are considering leaving the country, or have a relative who is, if Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is re-elected, according to a Channel 12 poll reported by The Times of Israel. The survey found that another 68 percent said they were not considering leaving the country, and the remaining respondents were unsure. The military said the interceptions caused no casualties or material damage, and Royal Engineers have begun clearing and securing debris from the impact sites. articles.title: Hormuz traffic falls to three-week low, MarineTraffic says
articles.summary: Hormuz traffic falls to three-week low, MarineTraffic says Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz fell to a three-week low of eight vessels on Thursday, down from 15 the previous day, ship-tracking platform MarineTraffic said. In a post on X, MarineTraffic said seven of the eight transits used the Iranian shipping lane, with no vessels crossing via the Omani route and no shadow fleet movements recorded. articles.title: Report: Israeli settlers spend full day inside occupied Syrian territory
articles.summary: Report: Israeli settlers spend full day inside occupied Syrian territory A group of Israeli settlers illegally crossed the border into Syria and spent a full day there before being apprehended by Israeli troops on Friday, according to the Times of Israel. This was the latest of several recent incidents in which settlers have entered the Syrian territory occupied by Israel’s army. The settlers, who had crossed the border near Majdal Shams, were handed over to the Israeli police for questioning, the Israeli military said, according to the TOI.

articles.title: IRIB: US missiles hit Larak Island maritime traffic control facility
articles.summary: IRIB: US missiles hit Larak Island maritime traffic control facility US missiles struck the central mast of the Ports and Maritime Organisation's maritime traffic control facility on Larak Island, according to Iran's state broadcaster IRIB. IRIB also reported that Iran's army said its Arash suicide drones targeted a US military ammunition depot at Camp Al-Udairi and ammunition storage facilities at Ali Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait. The army said several bridges in Kuwait that the US uses for military logistics were targeted in response to US attacks on Iran's transport bridges.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia says danger passed in Al-Kharj, Yanbu after 'potential danger' warnings
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia says danger passed in Al-Kharj, Yanbu after 'potential danger' warnings The Saudi civil defence issued two early warnings for Al-Kharj city and Yanbu early Saturday to be alert to "potential danger," but it later said the danger had passed in both areas, without providing details on the danger that triggered the warnings, according to Reuters. Iran targeted an American military base in Saudi Arabia with a ballistic missile, the first time that the Islamic Republic has directly attacked the kingdom in four months, a US official had told Axios.

articles.title: Israeli strike on funeral kills eight, wounds 22 in Gaza
articles.summary: Israeli strike on funeral kills eight, wounds 22 in Gaza Gaza's civil defence agency and a hospital said eight people were killed in an Israeli airstrike on a gathering of civilians in the Al-Balata market area of Nuseirat refugee camp. The area's Al-Awda hospital said it received eight dead and 22 people wounded in the strike. Mahmoud Bassal, spokesman for the civil defence agency, told AFP the strike occurred during a funeral procession. articles.summary: IRGC says US drone downed over Bushehr as projectiles hit Yazd Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy said it intercepted and shot down a US MQ-9 drone over the southern port city of Bushehr using a newly developed air defence system.
